channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Plunge into Python: New tools and tips for Python developers
item.description: Create luscious-looking full-stack websites in nothing but Python, get a hands-on lesson in integrating Rust and Python, and learn everything you need to know about the care and feeding of Python virtual environments.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Simple, secure device retirement
item.description: Is your IT service provider’s warehouse overrun by your depreciating end-of-life IT assets, and they don’t know what to do with them? Do your work-from-home and on-the-go employees have old devices lying around collecting dust and posing a security risk? Retire devices securely and responsibly—and a
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Bringing zero downtime to anywhere work
item.description: In a work-from-anywhere world, how can a team of so few be expected to keep doing so much for so many? The pace is unsustainable. It’s time for IT to enlist some support of its own and delegate—handing off time-consuming tasks to people and to intelligent, automated tools.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Ericsson provides 5G solutions to hybrid workforce
item.description: Founded as a telephony business in Stockholm in 1876, Ericsson is a provider of wireless and 5G services to telecoms operators worldwide and is currently investing heavily in cloud-based enterprise solutions. At the same time, the business’s basic principles remain unchanged.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Stay ahead of security threats to support hybrid work
item.description: HP Print Security Software Solutions provide simplified secure print management to the distributed workforce, reducing security risks, improving IT efficiencies, and enhancing employee experiences.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: How Apple Vision Pro is finding a home in healthcare
item.description: In early 2024, Ryan Broderick, a surgeon at UC San Diego Health, was commiserating with some colleagues about the profusion of screens in today’s operating rooms. Though the displays provided essential guidance for minimally invasive operations in progress, they also added complexity.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: Grim memories resurface after DNA advances lead to arrest of suspect in Hawaii teen's 1977 murder
item.description: The unsolved death of a teenager in 1977 haunted students and staff at Honolulu's McKinley High School for nearly half a century.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Sinco Kelleher, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Meta’s Q4 profits exceed Wall Street’s expectations
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. posted sharply higher profit and revenue for its fourth quarter on Wednesday, thanks to higher ad revenue on its social media properties, sending its shares up in after-hours trading even as it forecast increasing expenses on its artificial intelligence efforts.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Justice Department sues to block $14 billion Juniper buyout by Hewlett Packard Enterprise
item.description: U.S. government regulators sued to block Hewlett Packard Enterprise’s $14 billion acquisition of rival Juniper Networks on Thursday, saying the combination would eliminate competition, raise prices and reduce innovation.
item.dc:creator: Matt Ott, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Network Engineers See Value in AI, but Anticipate Barriers
item.description: Survey results show network engineers see value in using artificial intelligence for network management and cybersecurity purposes, but anticipate more barriers to successful adoption than their C-suite counterparts.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Vodafone makes 'world's first' satellite video call with a standard phone –here's why that's a big deal
item.description: Vodafone has announced that it's made one of the first satellite video calls using a standard phone – ahead of a full rollout later this year.
item.dc:creator: mark.wilson@futurenet.com (Mark Wilson), TechRadar

item.title: Stranger Things season 5's 12-month shoot yielded 650-plus hours of footage for its eight 'blockbuster movie' episodes, the popular Netflix show's creators say
item.description: Stranger Things' creators shot a head-spinning amount of footage for the hugely successful show's fifth and final season.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: RX 9070 GPU could theoretically be an RTX 5070 killer, I’m just worried that AMD may not go for Nvidia’s throat with pricing
item.description: AMD’s RX 9070 XT could outgun Nvidia’s RTX 5070 Ti easily, and come within 10% of the RTX 5080 GPU, if fresh rumors prove correct.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Netflix reveals June 2025 release date for Squid Game season 3, and its first clip teases a new mini-game that'll have a big impact on Gi-hun and his fellow contestants
item.description: Squid Game's third and final season will be with us sooner than you think – and its first-look images have got me worried for Gi-hun's safety.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: NASA's 2 stuck astronauts take their first spacewalk together
item.description: NASA's two stuck astronauts are taking their first spacewalk together, exiting the International Space Station almost eight months after moving in.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: I was excited by Netflix’s Black Doves renewal, but Ben Whishaw’s disappointing season 2 update means we likely won't see it this year
item.description: I was excited to hear that Black Doves had been renewed, but Ben Whishaw's season 2 update has left me disappointed.
item.dc:creator: grace.morris@futurenet.com (Grace Morris), TechRadar

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg just teased next-gen Ray-Ban smart glasses – here are 4 things I want to see
item.description: Mark Zuckerberg has convinced me we’re getting next-gen Ray-Ban smart glasses this year – here are 4 things I want to see from them.
item.dc:creator: hamish.hector@futurenet.com (Hamish Hector), TechRadar

item.title: Nvidia RTX 5090 GPU stock rumored to be ‘basically non-existent’ for launch day, and RTX 5080 doesn’t sound like it’ll be much better
item.description: One major retailer claims it still has no stock of the RTX 5090 GPU, at all, on the evening before launch
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Trump World learns how to deal with Musk
item.description: President Trump’s allies and aides are learning to deal with Tesla CEO Elon Musk, who has had a consistent presence in the early days of the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: The Google Pixel 9a will reportedly ship in less than two months, and we also have an idea of when it might be announced
item.description: Pixel 9a pre-order and shipping dates have been tipped, giving us an idea of when the phone might be announced too.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Alibaba introduces Qwen 2.5-Max AI model, claims edge over DeepSeek
item.description: China’s Alibaba Group has launched an upgraded version of its Qwen 2.5 AI model, claiming it outperforms models from DeepSeek, OpenAI, and Meta, as competition in the AI market intensifies.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How execs can bridge the AI knowledge gap
item.description: From streamlining administrative tasks to enhancing brainstorming sessions, AI is becoming an essential workplace companion. Yet, despite its transformative promise, its integration isn’t as simple as flipping a switch.
item.dc:creator: Dave Grow, Fast Company

item.title: Meta posts sharply higher Q4 profit, revenue, topping Wall Street's expectations
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. posted sharply higher profit and revenue for its fourth quarter on Wednesday, thanks to higher ad revenue on its social media properties.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Did DeepSeek copy ChatGPT to make new AI chatbot? Trump adviser thinks so
item.description: Did the upstart Chinese tech company DeepSeek copy ChatGPT to make the artificial intelligence technology that shook Wall Street this week? That's what ChatGPT maker OpenAI is suggesting, along with U.S. President Donald Trump's top AI adviser.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: AI-driven power boom will drive demand 38% higher on top US grid
item.description: Power usage in PJM and the U.S. more broadly is expected to climb at an unprecedented clip as data centers used to run artificial intelligence proliferate and become much larger.
item.dc:creator: Tribune News Service, The Mercury News

item.title: Are we all aliens? NASA's returned asteroid samples hold the ingredients of life from a watery world
item.description: A NASA spacecraft has returned asteroid samples that hold not only the pristine building blocks for life but also the salty remains of an ancient water world.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: iPhones on T-Mobile can access Starlink satellite communications
item.description: Starlink support appears to be a secret iOS 18.3 feature for iPhones, some of which now support satellite communication services from SpaceX — but only for users invited to join public beta testing of the service on US carrier T-Mobile.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The AI bust is here
item.description: Hands up! Who remembers the dot.com bust? I do. It was March 10, 2000. The NASDAQ Composite index peaked at 5,048.62 points. That doesn’t sound like much today, but it was up 800% from 1995. Then people started to get nervous about the true value of the dot.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 3 exceptional text expansion tools for Windows
item.description: Do you find yourself typing the same things over and over? If the answer is yes — and if you type enough text during the workweek, it almost certainly will be — I’d highly recommend you install a text expansion application.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Windows 11: A guide to the updates
item.description: A Windows launch isn’t the end a process — it’s really just the beginning. Microsoft continually works on improving Windows 11 by fixing bugs, releasing security patches, and occasionally adding new features.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Doing authentication right
item.description: Any substantial website is going to need to customize itself for individual users. Thus, it is going to have to authenticate those users — that is, let them log in. As a software developer, it is your job to make the whole process as smooth and secure as possible.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: DashCon, the infamous celebration of Tumblr fandom, is coming back. This time, organizers say it will be different
item.description: Anyone well-versed in internet lore will be familiar with the image of a slightly deflated child’s ball pit on a hotel convention room floor. DashCon (originally called Tumblr-Con, although not officially affiliated with Tumblr) was a fan convention held in Schaumburg, Illinois, back in 2014.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Now US government agencies can use OpenAI’s ChatGPT too
item.description: Everyone wants to get their hands on AI tools, and employees in government agencies are no exception. Still, stakes are far higher for governments when it comes to security, privacy, and protecting the public.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Trump asks Musk to 'go get' NASA astronauts stuck in space
item.description: President Trump said Tuesday that he “asked Elon Musk and @SpaceX to ‘go get’ the 2 brave astronauts who have been virtually abandoned in space.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: UK regulator may investigate AWS and Microsoft cloud businesses
item.description: AWS and Microsoft could potentially face an investigation of their cloud businesses in Britain should the Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) follow the recommendation of an independent inquiry group that reports into the UK’s antitrust regulator.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: New Vatican document offers AI guidelines from warfare to health care
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — A Vatican document released Tuesday offers wide-ranging ethical guidelines for the application of artificial intelligence in sectors from warfare to health care, with an underlying call that the burgeoning technology must be used as a tool to complement, and not replace, human in
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4
